capture.title: Capture
capture.description: Periodically record the focused window and a screenshot, so you can review activity later.
capture.enableDescription: When off, window info and screenshots stop being recorded.
capture.intervalDescription: How often window info and screenshots are captured.
capture.intervalHint:
Screenshots trigger when:
• Switching apps → captured immediately
• Browser changes URL → captured immediately
• Same window stays focused for the configured interval → captured
capture.idleDescription: Stop counting time after prolonged mouse/keyboard inactivity.
capture.idleHint:
Detection uses system-level mouse / keyboard / trackpad events.
Set to 0 to disable (never trigger).
capture.dataPathHint: Location of the database and the default screenshots root. Changing requires a restart, and old data must be migrated manually.

workHours.title: Work hours
workHours.description: Only capture during the specified hours, so off-hours activity isn't recorded. Multiple ranges supported.

startup.title: Startup behavior
startup.autoStartDescription: Hindsight starts automatically after you log in.
startup.showWindowDescription: When off, the app stays in the tray and you open it manually when needed.
startup.minimizeToTrayDescriptionMac: Clicking the X in the top-left of the window hides it to the tray; capture and sync keep running in the background. When off, clicking X quits the app.
startup.minimizeToTrayDescriptionWin: Clicking the X in the top-right of the window hides it to the tray; capture and sync keep running in the background. When off, clicking X quits the app.

dataRootDialog.title: Switch data folder?

storage.title: Storage
storage.description: Screenshot retention and current usage. The database is never auto-cleaned.
storage.retentionDescription: Expired screenshot files are deleted automatically (activity records are kept).
storage.modelsPathHint: After changing the path, models in the old folder are not migrated — copy them over manually, or re-download them in the new location.

manage.title: Data management
manage.description: Clean up unwanted data to free disk space.
manage.purgeDbDescription: Delete all window activity records. Categories and settings are kept.

purgeDialog.dbMessage: All window activity records will be deleted. Categories and settings are unaffected. This cannot be undone.
purgeDialog.shotsMessage: All saved screenshot files will be deleted. The corresponding screenshot references in the database will also be cleared. This cannot be undone.

browser.title: Browser filter
browser.info: Skip screenshots when the browser address bar URL contains any of these (case-insensitive).

app.title: App filter
app.info: Skip screenshots when the app name or window title contains any of these (case-insensitive).

scope.title: Scope
scope.info: When matched, only screenshots are skipped
scope.noticePrefix: Screenshot filtering is a local-only behavior,
scope.noticeEmph: it does not affect screenshots already saved
scope.noticeMiddle: . To clear historical screenshots, go to

update.title: App updates

status.installing: Downloading and installing — the app will restart when done…

update.autoDescription: When enabled, the app silently checks at startup at the frequency below and notifies you if a new version is available.

timeRangeList.empty: No ranges set — capturing all day

segmentList.empty: No segments yet — click "Add segment" below to start
segmentList.swatchAutoTitle: Auto-gradient based on hour of day

categories.title: App Categories
categories.intro: Group processes into activity categories. Color, icon, name, and bound apps are all customizable.

deleteDialog.title: Delete category
deleteDialog.message: Delete category "{{name}}"? Its apps will be returned to "Other".

pairing.infoTipBody: The same app may have different process names on different systems ("Code" on macOS, "Visual Studio Code" on Windows). Without pairing, time is counted as two separate apps and the category has to be set on each device. Once paired, the name, category, icon, and time are unified.
pairing.instructionSuffix: to move the same app onto one row — unifying name, category, and merged time.
pairing.unassignedHint: Unassigned rows are highlighted in yellow
pairing.noDevices: No device data yet. Check back after capturing for a while.

engine.sectionDesc: Hindsight ships with the llama.cpp inference engine and runs everything locally — screenshots are never uploaded to any external service.

noCuda.headline: NVIDIA CUDA was not detected.
noCuda.prefix: Vision LLMs run very slowly on CPU. We recommend installing the
noCuda.suffix: (≥ 12.4) first; Hindsight will automatically switch to the GPU-accelerated variant after a restart.

engine.uninstallConfirm: Uninstall the local AI engine? Model files will not be affected.

actions.testTooltipReady: Start the engine (if not running) and verify by hitting the local /v1/models endpoint
actions.openFolderTooltipInstalled: Reveal the install folder in the file manager

models.sectionDesc: Vision LLM used for local inference; GGUF files are downloaded from HuggingFace.

card.inUseTooltip: Click to deactivate and return to 'Downloaded' (the running server will be stopped)
card.readyTooltip: Click to activate this model (the running server will be stopped; restart manually to load the new model)

models.uninstallConfirm: Uninstall {{name}}? This will delete the main weights{{extra}} — cannot be undone.

expand.busyTooltip: Can collapse only after downloads finish

brief.sectionInfo: Included in AI summaries to help the model understand your work and context.
brief.placeholder: e.g. I'm a backend developer who mostly writes Rust and TypeScript; on weekends I tinker with games.

prompt.sectionDesc: Tell the model how to write summaries. Each language has a built-in default; click Save after editing, or Reset to revert to default.

actions.resetTooltip: Refill the editor with the built-in default (click Save to apply)
actions.saveTooltip: Save the override for the current language

segments.sectionInfo: AI summaries are produced per segment; screenshots within each segment are sampled by similarity before being sent to the model.

filter.rowHint:
Click to toggle:
• Colored + category icon = included in AI analysis
• Gray outline + closed-eye icon = excluded

frame.sectionDesc: Screenshots can pile up over time — deduplicate by similarity first, then send the rest to the model to save time and tokens.
frame.thresholdHint: How different two screenshots can be and still count as the same. Lower is stricter — set to 0 to only treat pixel-identical frames as duplicates and exclude them from analysis.
frame.windowHint: Only compare screenshots taken within this window, so similar frames from different parts of the day don't get merged.

devices.title: Devices
devices.meta: Manage this device's name and the sync status of other devices.
devices.emptyOthers: Sign in with the same account on another device to see it here.

desc.signInPrompt: Sign in with Google to sync activity data across devices via Drive. Screenshots are never uploaded.
desc.configurePrompt: Before first use, create an OAuth credential in Google Cloud Console (about 3 minutes).

actions.reauthTitle: Credentials expired — sign in again to refresh the token

cloud.restartHint: Signed in to a new account. Restart the app to switch to that account's local database.

errors.credExpired: Credentials expired. Please sign in again.

step2.title: Configure the OAuth consent screen
step2.bodyMiddle: → fill in any app name and email → set Audience to External → enter your email again under contact info → click

step3.title: Create the OAuth client
step3.bodyPrefix: Open Clients on the left → click
step3.bodySuffix: at the top → pick Desktop app as the application type → use any name. The Client ID and Client Secret will then be shown.

step4.title: Paste the Client ID and Client Secret below
